comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review advisory triage (PASS, 0 blocking, 19 advisory). Actionable resolved in design: (A adv1/3) verify(kind, envelope, trust_root): envelope=dsse.Envelope, trust_root=caller-supplied per-scheme trust material (opaque). (B adv2/6/7) namespace = domain-separation string the policy pins and passes to the scheme; test asserts scheme invoked with policy-pinned namespace. (C adv4/5) DELIBERATE split: registry pins security-critical {scheme,namespace} (the dispatch key, never read from envelope -> anti alg-confusion); trust material is caller-supplied (which keys to trust is a deployment concern). (D adv9/11/12) POLICY is a hardcoded module-level dict (source-pinned, NOT config/env-loaded - that IS the security property); schemes register into a module dict via register_scheme(). (E adv10) unknown kind -> fail closed (raise/fail verdict), tested. (F adv13) added corrupt-signature verify-failure test via a fake scheme. (adv8) file_impact set. (adv17) resolved: 9fd5 attest package now committed. JUSTIFIED-INVALID (severity none, impact 0.0): adv14/15/16/18/19 argue premature-abstraction/rule-of-three/already-solved -> the decision to build ONE shared asymmetric substrate (vs duplicating security-critical crypto across the identity + op-cert epics) was made and plan-reviewed at the EPIC level (2fd4 Context); this leaf implements that approved decision, not re-litigate it. Re-signing after file_impact change.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
The domain-separation core: a registry mapping each attestation `kind` to `{scheme, namespace, trust-root}`, pinned out-of-band, so the verifier never selects the scheme from attacker-controlled record data (anti alg-confusion, RFC 8725).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/rebar/attest/registry.py` resolves `kind -> {scheme, namespace, trust_root}` from a static policy table, not from the envelope.
- [ ] `verify(kind, envelope, trust_root)` dispatches on the policy scheme; a test asserts a record whose in-band scheme/keyid is tampered still verifies under the policy-pinned scheme.
- [ ] Unknown/mismatched scheme for a kind fails closed (test).

file_impact.reason: scheme registry + per-kind policy table + verify-dispatch
dispatch, alg-confusion, unknown-kind/scheme fail-closed, namespace-passthrough tests

compiled_state.title: attest: scheme registry + per-kind policy table + verify-dispatch
